page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg5.3xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6149 processor. It features 12 physical cores and 12 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The instance is configured with 48.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as the stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1355.89 and the Geekbench single-core score of 1100.0, fall into the bottom 25% tier. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 5452.0 and a PassMark CPU score of 11929.55. Memory bandwidth is stable, recording 603.79 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 56.31 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads.

This instance is suited for workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores but do not require high single-threaded performance. It achieves average results in database operations and Redis SET operations, making it viable for moderate database hosting and web serving. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the lack of bundled local storage and the modest single-core performance suggest it is best utilized for steady-state, multi-threaded general-purpose workloads rather than bursty or single-thread-heavy tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg5.3xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance featuring 12 dedicated physical cores and 12 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6149 processor running at 3.1 GHz. It is equipped with 48.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate weak single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1100.0, but average multi-core scaling, as evidenced by a PassMark CPU score of 11929.55 and a Redis SET throughput of 10.75 million operations per second. This hardware profile is suited for multi-threaded workloads, moderate database operations, and general-purpose web serving that do not demand high single-threaded performance or local NVMe storage.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg5.3xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 12 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 48 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps bandwidth, delivering average multi-core and moderate database performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg5.3xlarge features 12 dedicated Intel Xeon cores and 48 GB RAM, offering balanced multi-core performance for general workloads.
tagline: A dedicated twelve-core x86_64 instance with balanced memory allocation, delivering steady multi-threaded performance for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 12 dedicated physical Intel Xeon cores running at 3.1 GHz
48.0 GB of system memory with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Average multi-core performance suitable for parallel processing tasks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the configuration
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on a KVM hypervisor
Best suited for moderate database operations and general web serving
